1.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
1.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

1.gl: to occupy (by driving out previous tenants, and possessing in their place); by implication, to seize, to rob, to inherit; also to expel, to impoverish, to ruin
1.kj: cast out, consume, destroy, disinherit, dispossess, drive(-ing) out, enjoy, expel, [idiom] without fail, (give to, leave for) inherit(-ance, -or) [phrase] magistrate, be (make) poo

1.gl: to strike (lightly or severely, literally or figuratively)
1.kj: beat, cast forth, clap, give (wounds), [idiom] go forward, [idiom] indeed, kill, make (slaughter), murderer, punish, slaughter, slay(-er, -ing), smite(-r, -ing), strike, be stricke

1.gl: a stream, especially a winter torrent; (by implication) a (narrow) valley (in which a brook runs); also a shaft (of a mine)
1.dv: or (feminine) נַחְלָה; (Psalm 124:4), or נַחֲלָה; (Ezekiel 47:19; Ezekiel 48:28), from H5157 (נָחַל) in its original sense;

1.gl: properly, a region across; but used only adverbially (with or without a preposition) on the opposite side (especially of the Jordan; ususally meaning the east)
1.kj: [idiom] against, beyond, by, [idiom] from, over, passage, quarter, (other, this) side, straight.

1.gl: a desert; especially (with the article prefix) the (generally) sterile valley of the Jordan and its continuation to the Red Sea
1.kj: Arabah, champaign, desert, evening, heaven, plain, wilderness. See also H1026 (בֵּית הָעֲרָבָה).
1.dv: from H6150 (עָרַב) (in the sense of sterility);

2.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
2.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

2.gl: properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
2.kj: (make to) abide(-ing), continue, (cause to, make to) dwell(-ing), ease self, endure, establish, [idiom] fail, habitation, haunt, (make to) inhabit(-ant), make to keep (house), lurk

2.gl: the lip (as a natural boundary); by implication, language; by analogy, a margin (of a vessel, water, cloth, etc.)
2.kj: band, bank, binding, border, brim, brink, edge, language, lip, prating, (sea-)shore, side, speech, talk, (vain) words.
2.dv: or (in dual and plural) שֶׂפֶת; probably from H5595 (סָפָה) or H8192 (שָׁפָה) through the idea of termination (compare H5490 (סוֹף));

2.gl: a stream, especially a winter torrent; (by implication) a (narrow) valley (in which a brook runs); also a shaft (of a mine)
2.dv: or (feminine) נַחְלָה; (Psalm 124:4), or נַחֲלָה; (Ezekiel 47:19; Ezekiel 48:28), from H5157 (נָחַל) in its original sense;

2.gl: a bisection, i.e. (by implication) the centre
2.kj: among(-st), [idiom] between, half, [idiom] (there-, where-), in(-to), middle, mid(-night), midst (among), [idiom] out (of), [idiom] through, [idiom] with(-in).
2.dv: from an unused root meaning to sever;

2.gl: properly, a cord (as twisted), i.e. (by implication) a boundary; by extension the territory inclosed
2.kj: border, bound, coast, [idiom] great, landmark, limit, quarter, space.

3.gl: the bottom (as depressed); only adverbially, below (often with prepositional prefix underneath), in lieu of, etc.
3.kj: as, beneath, [idiom] flat, in(-stead), (same) place (where...is), room, for...sake, stead of, under, [idiom] unto, [idiom] when...was mine, whereas, (where-) fore, with.

3.gl: a sea (as breaking in noisy surf) or large body of water; specifically (with the article), the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river, or an artifical basin; locally, the west, or (rarely) the south
3.dv: from an unused root meaning to roar;

3.gl: a desert; especially (with the article prefix) the (generally) sterile valley of the Jordan and its continuation to the Red Sea
3.kj: Arabah, champaign, desert, evening, heaven, plain, wilderness. See also H1026 (בֵּית הָעֲרָבָה).
3.dv: from H6150 (עָרַב) (in the sense of sterility);

3.gl: a road (as trodden); figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb
3.kj: along, away, because of, [phrase] by, conversation, custom, (east-) ward, journey, manner, passenger, through, toward, (high-) (path-) way(-side), whither(-soever).

3.gl: Kinneroth or Kinnereth, a place in Palestine
3.dv: or כִּנֶּרֶתlemma כִּנֶרֶת missing dagesh, corrected to כִּנֶּרֶת; respectively plural and singular feminine from the same as H3658 (כִּנּוֹר); perhap

3.gl: Beth-ha-Jeshimoth, a town East of the Jordan
3.dv: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3451 (יְשִׁימַה) with the article interposed; house of the deserts;

4.gl: properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
4.kj: (make to) abide(-ing), continue, (cause to, make to) dwell(-ing), ease self, endure, establish, [idiom] fail, habitation, haunt, (make to) inhabit(-ant), make to keep (house), lurk

4.gl: properly, an overhanging, i.e. (by implication) an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope (as hanging free)
4.kj: [phrase] abundant, cord, exceeding, excellancy(-ent), what they leave, that hath left, plentifully, remnant, residue, rest, string, with.

4.gl: Ashtaroth, the name of a Sidonian deity, and of a place East of the Jordan

4.gl: properly, a cord (as twisted), i.e. (by implication) a boundary; by extension the territory inclosed
4.kj: border, bound, coast, [idiom] great, landmark, limit, quarter, space.
giant, Rapha, Rephaim(-s). See also H1051 (בֵּית רָפָא).
4.dv: or רָפָה; from H7495 (רָפָא) in the sense of invigorating;

4.gl: Edrei, the name of two places in Palestine
4.dv: from the equivalent of H153 (אֶדְרָע); mighty;

5.gl: properly, a cord (as twisted), i.e. (by implication) a boundary; by extension the territory inclosed
5.kj: border, bound, coast, [idiom] great, landmark, limit, quarter, space.

5.gl: a mountain or range of hills (sometimes used figuratively)

5.kj: (have, make to have) dominion, governor, [idiom] indeed, reign, (bear, cause to, have) rule(-ing, -r), have power.
half, middle, mid(-night), midst, part, two parts.
5.dv: from H2673 (חָצָה)lemma חָעָה ayin, corrected to חָצָה;
